briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposed AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware firm Synapse, highlighting critical risks in bank-fintech partnerships.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly unconventional credit card products, blurring the lines between traditional lending and embedded finance.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government declared screen scraping a security risk and committed to a timeline for implementing a formal open banking framework — a foundational moment for Canada's financial data future.

- **[How the war in the Middle East is already impacting mortgage rates in Canada](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/iran-war-mortgage-rates-canada-9.7152182?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Rising geopolitical tensions tied to the US-Iran conflict are pushing up Canadian bond yields, with ripple effects now being felt in domestic mortgage rates.

- **[How new SR&ED changes could supercharge Canadian hard tech and manufacturing startups](https://betakit.com/how-new-sred-changes-could-supercharge-canadian-hard-tech-and-manufacturing-startups/)** — *BetaKit* — Proposed reforms to Canada's Scientific Research and Experimental Development tax credit program could unlock significant new capital for Canadian tech and innovation startups.

- **[BDC launches $150-million life sciences fund](https://betakit.com/bdc-launches-150-million-life-sciences-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada has launched a new $150-million fund targeting life sciences ventures, reflecting continued federal appetite for strategic startup investment.

- **[Digital Asset Technologies Inc. Announces Intention to Amend and Refile Financial Statements and MD&A](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/digital-asset-technologies-inc-announces-intention-to-amend-and-refile-financial-statements-and-mda)** — *Financial Post* — Vancouver-based Digital Asset Technologies Inc. (CSE: DATT) has announced plans to amend and refile its financial statements, raising transparency and governance questions for Canadian digital asset investors.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Blackline Safety to go private in $850-million deal](https://betakit.com/blackline-safety-to-go-private-in-850-million-usd-deal/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian tech firm Blackline Safety is set to go private in an $850-million USD deal, a notable example of venture-backed Canadian tech exits that offers lessons for Atlantic Canadian ecosystem builders on exit pathways and investor appetite.

- **[Canada's spotty support for innovation is grounding its space ambitions](https://betakit.com/canadas-spotty-support-for-innovation-is-grounding-its-space-ambitions/)** — *BetaKit* — A BetaKit analysis critiques Canada's inconsistent approach to funding innovation sectors, a challenge that resonates strongly with Atlantic Canadian fintech founders navigating limited regional capital and federal support programs.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This emerging critical perspective on fintech's social consequences is a timely provocation for Atlantic Canadian ecosystem leaders focused on building inclusive, community-centred financial innovation.
